title: Dongri Fort
tagline: Dongri Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Dongri Fort, or the Dongri Hill Fort, locally known as the Janjire Dhavari Fort, is a fort in Mumbai, India.
overview.description: The Dongri Fort, or the Dongri Hill Fort, locally known as the Janjire Dhavari Fort, is a fort in Mumbai, India. It is located in the Dongri area. It came under Maratha rule in 1739. Since then, the locals and the church have been looking after the maintenance of the fort, which was once repaired.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Dongri
topPlaces.description: Dongri is a port city in the Mira-Bhayandar municipality of Thane district, situated just north of Gorai, Mumbai. The word 'Dungaree' (worker's overalls worn in Britain) has its origin in Dongri due to this genre of garment monolithically being manufactured from textile sectors within the confines of the city.

topPlaces.name: Uttan
topPlaces.description: Uttan (Marathi: [utːən]) is a coastal town just north of Mumbai in the Thane district of Maharashtra, India. A coastal town, it falls under the jurisdiction of the Mira-Bhayandar Municipal Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Fort Vasai
topPlaces.description: Fort Vasai (Vasai killa in Marathi, Fort Vasai in English) is a ruined fort in the town of Vasai (Bassein), Konkan Division, Maharashtra, India. The structure was formally christened as the Fort of St.

topPlaces.name: Ulhas River
topPlaces.description: The Ulhas River is a west-flowing river in Maharashtra, India. It is in the Thane, Raigad, and Pune districts of that state.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Vasai
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Vasai (Latin: Vasaien(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Vasai in the ecclesiastical province of Bombay in India.

topPlaces.name: Vadavali
topPlaces.description: Vadavali is a village in the Palghar district of Maharashtra, India. It is located in the Talasari taluka.

topPlaces.name: Bhayandar
topPlaces.description: Bhayandar, also spelled as Bhaindar or Bhayander, is a suburb located in the western suburbs of Mumbai and the northern most suburb of the island on the western side, near the Vasai Creek. It has a large Marathi, Aagri, Koli, and mixed-community population.

topPlaces.name: Mira-Bhayandar
topPlaces.description: Mira-Bhayandar is a city and municipal corporation in Thane district in the state of Maharashtra, India located in the northern part of Salsette Island before the Vasai Creek, and shares a border with North Mumbai. Mira-Bhayandar is administered by Mira-Bhayandar Municipal Corporation (MBMC), with a population at the 2011 Census of 809,378.

items.note: Dongri is a port city in the Mira-Bhayandar municipality of Thane district, situated just north of Gorai, Mumbai. The word 'Dungaree' (worker's overalls worn in Britain) has its origin in Dongri due to this genre of garment monolithically being manufactured from textile sectors within the confines of the city.

Uttan (Marathi: [utːən]) is a coastal town just north of Mumbai in the Thane district of Maharashtra, India. A coastal town, it falls under the jurisdiction of the Mira-Bhayandar Municipal Corporation.

Fort Vasai (Vasai killa in Marathi, Fort Vasai in English) is a ruined fort in the town of Vasai (Bassein), Konkan Division, Maharashtra, India. The structure was formally christened as the Fort of St.
The Ulhas River is a west-flowing river in Maharashtra, India. It is in the Thane, Raigad, and Pune districts of that state.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Vasai (Latin: Vasaien(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Vasai in the ecclesiastical province of Bombay in India.
Vadavali is a village in the Palghar district of Maharashtra, India. It is located in the Talasari taluka.

Bhayandar, also spelled as Bhaindar or Bhayander, is a suburb located in the western suburbs of Mumbai and the northern most suburb of the island on the western side, near the Vasai Creek. It has a large Marathi, Aagri, Koli, and mixed-community population.

Mira-Bhayandar is a city and municipal corporation in Thane district in the state of Maharashtra, India located in the northern part of Salsette Island before the Vasai Creek, and shares a border with North Mumbai. Mira-Bhayandar is administered by Mira-Bhayandar Municipal Corporation (MBMC), with a population at the 2011 Census of 809,378.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dongri Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dongri Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dongri Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (36 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dongri Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dongri Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dongri Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Dongri Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dongri Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dongri, Uttan, Fort Vasai, Ulhas River, Diocese of Vasai.
